2606.20485.title: Optimal Order of Multi-Agent and General Many-Body Systems
2606.20485.paper_summary: This paper develops a general framework for analyzing multi-agent systems with feedback loops between agents actions and collective observations. The framework is built on two fundamental agent-level variables: power, which measures agent influence on collective outcomes, and response functions, which determine how agents react to observations. We derive how macroscopic properties, including total power, useful power, entropy, order, fragility, and mobility, emerge from these two variables of heterogeneous agents. To study the trade off between growth and resilience, we introduce a system-level utility function parameterized by a risk-appetite coefficient and derive an optimal degree of order that balances productivity, stability, and adaptability. The analysis suggests that stronger synchronization can increase collective output but may also increase systemic fragility and reduce mobility. We further argue that order, entropy, information, and useful energy are task-dependent and system-relative concepts whose meanings depend on the objectives of the system. By measuring and designing agent power distributions and response functions, it may be possible to better understand, predict, and optimize collective behavior and identify the conditions under which collective intelligence and optimal order emerge.
2606.20485.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文發展了一個分析具有反饋迴路的多代理系統的通用框架，該系統中代理的行動與集體觀察之間存在互動。<lb/>該框架建立在兩個基本的代理層級變數上：權力，這衡量代理對集體結果的影響，以及反應函數，這決定了代理如何對觀察做出反應。<lb/>我們推導出宏觀特性，包括總權力、有用權力、熵、秩序、脆弱性和流動性，如何從這兩個異質代理的變數中產生。<lb/>為了研究增長與韌性之間的權衡，我們引入了一個系統層級的效用函數，該函數由風險偏好係數參數化，並推導出一個最佳的秩序程度，以平衡生產力、穩定性和適應性。<lb/>分析表明，更強的同步性可以增加集體產出，但也可能增加系統的脆弱性並減少流動性。<lb/>我們進一步主張，秩序、熵、信息和有用能量是依賴於任務和系統的相對概念，其意義取決於系統的目標。<lb/>通過測量和設計代理的權力分佈和反應函數，可能更好地理解、預測和優化集體行為，並識別集體智慧和最佳秩序出現的條件。
2606.20485.author: Jake J. Xia et.al.
